State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 867 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1160 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95314

Technical Assistance • • Administration • • Executive/Legal • • Enforcement • • Statements of Economic Interest
(916) 322 5662 322 5660 322 5901 322 6441 327 6444

December 9, 1982


David H. Anderson
Attorney at Law
1114 State Street, Suite 310
Santa Barbara, CA 93101

Re: Our No. A-82-195

Dear Mr. Anderson:

Thank you for your letter of November 24, 1982,
regarding the Committee for a Responsible Council. In
answer to your question, the Political Reform Act does not
regulate names used by committees. There is nothing in the
Act which prohibits one committee from using a name which
is similar to that of another committee. Although the
Secretary of State will not register a committee under the
same name that has been used by another committee, it does
accept committee names which are similar to the names of
other committees.

In view of the fact that use of committees' names is
not controlled by the Political Reform Act, I cannot advise
you further as to what, if any, legal remedies you may have
under other laws. However, I hope I have clarified the
situation under the Political Reform Act for you.

Very truly yours,

Barbara A. Milman
Chief
Legal Division

State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814
Advice • (916) 322-5660 • • • Administration • • • Executive/Legal • • Enforcement • • Statement of Economic Interest
(916) 322 5665         322 5660             322 5901           322 5441            372 6444

November 30, 1982


David H. Anderson
Attorney at Law
1114 State Street, Suite 310
Santa Barbara, CA  93101

Re:  A-82-195

Dear Mr. Anderson:

     Your letter requesting advice under the Political
Reform Act has been referred to me.  If you have any
questions about your advice request, please contact me
directly at (916) 322-5901.

     We try to answer all advice requests promptly.
Therefore, unless your request poses particularly complex
legal questions, or unless more information is needed to
answer your request, you should expect a response within 21
working days.

Very truly yours,

Barbara A. Milman
Chief
Legal Division

BAM:plh
Enclosure

DAVID H. ANDERSON
ATTORNEY AT LAW

LA ARCADA BUILDING

ENVIRONMENTAL LAW
PLANNING & ZONING LAW
ADMINISTRATIVE LAW

1114 STATE STREET, SUITE 310
SANTA BARBARA, CALIFORNIA 93101
(805) 963-3588

November 24, 1982

Ms. Janis Shank McLean
Counsel
Legal Division
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, California 95804

Subject: Committee for a Responsible Council

Dear Ms. McLean:

I am submitting this letter as legal counsel for the Committee for
a Responsible Council. The Committee is a registered campaign
committee (ID #790078) which has been a very successful "grass
roots" organization of local homeowners who have backed City
Council candidates in the three most recent local elections. Six
of the seven current City Council members were supported by this
group, and endorsements by the Committee for a Responsible Council
carry a great deal of weight in our community.

During the most recent election campaign, a new group appeared
under the name Citizens for a Responsive Council. This group is
backed by certain persons who oppose the positions taken by the
Committee for a Responsible Council. The new group placed ads in
a local newspaper in opposition to a Charter Amendment which would
have increased the salary for City Council members. The Committee
for a Responsible Council did not take a position on that measure,
but a number of people in the community were confused by the
similarity of names and the measure was defeated. What role the
"copy-cat" group had in that defeat cannot be proven, but it had
some impact in confusing the voters.

The confusion created by the use of this "copy-cat" name may have
had an impact in reducing the effectiveness of endorsements by the
Committee for a Responsible Council in future elections - a result
which is exactly what the new group seeks.

My review of the Political Reform Act does not provide guidance on
this issue. The Committee for a Responsible Council would like to
take legal action to protect its registered name. I am therefore
requesting your assistance in advising the Committee if legal
remedies are available to prevent future voter confusion and
deception. Can the new group be required to change its name?
Can we enjoin the use of that name?

We understand that your office is presently inundated with complaints
and investigations arising from the most recent election campaigns.

DAVID H. ANDERSON
ATTORNEY AT LAW


Ms. Janis Shank McLean
November 24, 1982
Page Two

I am very appreciative of the guidance and assistance you provided
to the Santa Barbara County Counsel's Office in response to my
inquiry regarding the County Planning Commission. With the backlog
of other matters requiring your attention, we would appreciate any
informal guidance on how we might proceed with this issue, so that
we can take appropriate steps to prevent future voter deception.

Thank you very much for your assistance in this matter.


Sincerely,

David H. Anderson


cc: Alice Rypins
President, CRC
Raymond Myerson
Treasurer, CRC

Enclosures

City Council's
Charter Amendment #3

MEASURE
CHEATS
VOTERS

• Stops Voter Control
of City Council Salaries

• Allows unlimited Council
Salary increase in 1983

VOTE NO ON E

paid for by Citizens for a Responsive Council, Drawer I, S.B. 93102

AD CONFUSION
To the Editor:

An ad on page nine of your Oct. 28
issue urges a No vote on city Measure
E, "paid for by Citizens for a Respon-
sive Council." Using a name so similar
to Committee for a Responsible Coun-
cil appears to be an effort to confuse
the voters. Our group, the Committee
for a Responsive Council, took no pub-
lic stand on Measure E.

I have learned that Lee Scherfeld, a
politically astute council watcher re-
cently elected to the Board of Educa-
tion, was the signer of the check paying
for the ad. One would have hoped that
a member of that board would set a
better example of fairness for our chil-
dren.

I realize that because the News &
Review is a weekly, clarification won't

be possible before the Nov. 2 election.
Interestingly, the same ad in the News
Press, a daily, where this would have
been possible, did not include the cop-
ycat name.)

I feel confident that in the future, the
News & Review will be more alert for
ads from political mixflers.

Alice Rypins
President,
Committee for a Responsible Council.

RESPONSIVE

To the Editor:

Citizens for a Responsive Council is a
Recipient Committee, formed under Gov-
ernor Code Section 84101-84103, in
accordance with instructions of the Cali-
fornia Fair Political Practices Commis-
sion and having been issued Identification
No. 822862 by the Secretary of State.

As stated in the official filing, "The
purpose of the organization is to support
or oppose candidates and ballot mea-
sures with the goal of ensuring that
elected officials will be more responsive
to the wishes of the Community..."

The "Citizens" are pleased to have par-
ticipated in the opposition and resound-
ing defeat (NO 70.5%, YES 29.5%) of Mea-
sure E, Charter Amendment No. 3, and
express their sincere appreciation to all
who have helped.

Lee Scherfeld
Treasurer

NxR 11-11-82

sections.question_synthetic: Whether a registered campaign committee can prevent another organization from using a confusingly similar name that may deceive voters and reduce the effectiveness of the first committee's endorsements.
sections.parsing_notes: Format: ocr; LLM: This is an incoming request letter from an attorney, not an FPPC response. The document includes both the original request (November 24, 1982) and what appears to be FPPC acknowledgment correspondence with a referral notation (A-82-195). OCR quality is poor in some sections, affecting confidence slightly. No formal CONCLUSION section exists as this is the initial request, not the FPPC's response.

embedding.summary: Attorney David H. Anderson requests advice on behalf of the Committee for a Responsible Council (registered campaign committee #790078) regarding whether legal remedies exist to prevent another group, 'Citizens for a Responsive Council,' from using a confusingly similar name that may confuse voters and dilute the committee's political effectiveness.
